meta.title: Door Negotiation
meta.description: Mara Flint at the door of The Albion. Getting in is the first test.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: You playing, guesting, or hoping the room invents a reason for you to be here?

choices.text: Texts got crossed. I'm with Jules.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Expected by whom? The room doesn't send invitations. It sends rumours and sees who shows up dressed for them.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Fine. You've got the posture for it. Don't make me regret reading your coat instead of your name.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Jules is already three drinks into pretending tonight matters. Go find him before he starts quoting himself.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Jules didn't mention you. But Jules doesn't mention most things until they become anecdotes. In you go. Quietly.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Patience. That's either dignity or a very convincing impression of it. Alright. Stand over there. I'll find a gap.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: Seven minutes. Two cigarettes from strangers you don't smoke. Then Mara nods you through without looking up.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Bootsy knows everyone. That's his whole thing. But he did mention you might turn up looking like you weren't sure. So — consistent, at least.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Right. You're the one people are already talking about.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Try not to become tonight's administrative problem.

nodes.speaker: mara_flint
nodes.text: Mara steps aside. Not warmly. Not coldly. With the precise indifference of someone who has seen four hundred people walk through this door and remembers maybe six.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: The bass hits you in the sternum before your eyes adjust. The Albion is full.
